_doc: Declared (non-runnable) regimes for the #315 scalability table. These carry an honest status and NO fabricated runtime/peak-RSS numbers. Real, runnable rows come from run_metadata.json via emit_scalability_table.py; this file only adds the COMPUTE-wall (OOM) and DOWNLOAD-blocked regimes so the table tells the full scaling story (issue #315).

regimes.note: A single 124,938-cell graph makes the full-batch InfoNCE matrix O(n^2) ~= 232 GiB -> OOM by design. Minibatch (batch_size>0, #302) is required at this single-section scale; this is the architectural scaling wall, independent of any one dataset's biology (cf. the codex primary's 734k row runs full-batch cheaply because it is 565 small per-section graphs).
112k-699k cells; fetch-after-download (#300); minibatch-only at this scale.
